parsed.rationale: Region D displays a significantly higher concentration of fiber bundles compared to the other labeled areas. The fibers in this region are tightly packed and form a dense, coherent structure. In contrast, regions A, B, and C show more dispersed or less dense fiber arrangements. Therefore, Region D has the highest density of fiber bundles.
